learning_outcomes.description: Differentiate and adapt to audience and context.
Deploy writing techniques such as anecdotes, description, quotation.
Use journalistic investigation/research and editing skills at an advanced level.
Follow best practice protocols of ethical and professional journalism.
Engage in critical reflection and constructive critique of your own work and that of others.

assessments.due_date:
26/08/2026 12:30 pm

This is a paper-based exam that will take place during our lecture time. No outside material is permitted.
assessments.task_description:
This deadline newswriting task will be conducted in lecture time.

The task requires you to write a 300-word community news story from information provided in your class. 

You have 60 minutes to complete the task.

The story must take into account appropriate judgment and ethical choices that are covered in the first three weeks of lectures. The deadline is designed to replicate newsroom contexts.

Your story will be handwritten in class.

A deferred exam with take place two weeks later.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.task_description:
Produce a 3-6-minute video story on a civic issue. The civic journalism documentary must accurately and ethically cover a civic issue with creativity and emotion. Stories must be based in Brisbane.

All footage must be original. No AI or stock footage will be accepted. No pieces to camera will be accepted as this is a source driven story.

Students must submit full interview transcripts as well as information on where and when the interview took place.
assessments.submission_guidelines: This assignment will be submitted electronically via Blackboard and using EchoVideo.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
Write an open justice news story from a trip to the courts. The story must be:

  1. 600 words. 
  2. The story must conform to the fundamentals of civic journalism.
  3. You must have at least three primary sources in your story. In addition, you can use secondary sources. Primary sources must be interviewed face-to-face.
  4. Provide one image (photograph/illustration/artwork) to accompany the story. These must be your original work.
  5. You must include an appendix with a reference list of sources and contact details for the interviewee(s) and the date and manner in which the interviews took place. You must also include full transcripts of your interviews.
  6. Refer to the marking criteria in Blackboard.
  7. Stories must be based on visits to the courts no more than ten days before the due date.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.
